creator: peterj@FreeBSD.org
text:
Actually, having more carefully read the sources, statfs(2) doesn't update the cached values, only calling getfsstat(2) with MNT_WAIT works.  I think that's
even more of a POLA violation because it means that data returned by getfstatfs() can be older than data returned by statfs().

IMO, the logic in kern/vfs_mount.c:__vfs_statfs() should be inverted so that it always calls mp->mnt_op->vfs_statfs(mp, &mp->mnt_stat) and then copies the result to sbp if required, though I'm not sure of the subtleties that may be involved.
